title: Heliotropium lagoense
intro: Heliotropium lagoense, also known as Lagoon Heliotrope, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Heliotropium lagoense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat respiratory conditions and as a natural remedy for coughs and colds.
taxonomy: Heliotropium lagoense is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to Australia, specifically in Western Australia. Heliotropium lagoense is a shrub that grows up to 1 meter in height and is known for its small, blue or purple flowers. The classification of this plant includes being a member of the genus Heliotropium and the family Boraginaceae. Further classification includes being part of the order Borales, class Magnoliopsida, and division Magnoliophyta.
